column10:
Makor Care & Services Network is a Brooklyn-based non-profit organization serving individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities and their families across the lifespan. Founded in 1978, Makor is committed to promoting respect, inclusion, and dignity within the community.

Makor operates over 32 residential programs tailored to varying needs, from twelve-bed homes for children with profound disabilities and medical challenges to smaller apartments for higher-functioning adults, elderly individuals, and married couples. These provide 24/7 care and support.

Beyond residential services, Makor offers Early Intervention, Day Habilitation, Residential Habilitation, Respite Care, Medicaid Service Coordination, HCBS Waiver Programs, and Supported Employment. Their person-centered approach empowers participants to develop independence, vocational skills, and social connections.

The Makor College Experience, in partnership with Yeshiva University, provides young adults with intellectual disabilities academic enrichment, vocational training, and campus involvement in a non-degree program.

Combining professional expertise with compassionate care, Makor continues its mission to enhance quality of life and community integration for those they serve.

column10: o educate the public about the unmet needs of persons with serious mental illness who have anosognosia—a neurological symptom that leaves a person unable to understand that s/he is ill, resulting in treatment refusal, noncompliance, and conflict with loved ones and professionals. When you use LEAP -  Listen–Empathize–Agree–Partner®, you stop trying to convince the other person that they're wrong, or simply misguided. Instead, you listen in a new way that conveys respect for the person’s point of view and a complete lack of judgment. Sounds easy, but most people have a very hard time doing this simple thing. It is easy, once you learn LEAP®.

We are a resource center for parents of children with special needs. We have an extensive database of the services, programs, and professionals that service the special needs community (for example, but not limited to, ABA agencies, Sunday and after school programs, schools, camps and day camps, doctors, dentists, therapists, Board of Ed information, NY and NJ government programs, SSI, guardianship information, support groups, funds, medical supplies gemachim, and much more). We are not a referral center, but we have the personal touch that parents need when they call in. A live person to lend a listening ear and a guiding hand will speak with the parent until they have a clear picture of the options that suit their child's needs. We are constantly collecting references for programs and professionals so that we recommend only the best. We work closely with the parent and follow up until their concerns have been resloved. We also have a network of volunteer and paid babysitters for emergency respite, and we do our best to make arrangements to care for the special child when there's a simcha, a vacation, or a family emergency.

We are a resource center for parents of children with special needs. We have an extensive database of the services, programs, and professionals that service the special needs community (for example, but not limited to, ABA agencies, Sunday and after school programs, schools, camps and day camps, doctors, dentists, therapists, Board of Ed information, NY and NJ government programs, SSI, guardianship information, support groups, funds, medical supplies gemachim, and much more). We are not a referral center, but we have the personal touch that parents need when they call in. A live person to lend a listening ear and a guiding hand will speak with the parent until they have a clear picture of the options that suit their child's needs. We are constantly collecting references for programs and professionals so that we recommend only the best. We work closely with the parent and follow up until their concerns have been resloved. We also have a network of volunteer and paid babysitters for emergency respite, and we do our best to make arrangements to care for the special child when there's a simcha, a vacation, or a family emergency.

column10: Dedicated to helping you understand your past, so you can create a better future. Focused on helping members of the Jewish community understand the connections between the past and the present, including unresolved childhood issues, neglect, trauma, and abuse; and create a life of meaning. Also providing our Flagship program, Fresh Start Retreat, is a 7-day intensive retreat helping participants understand their past to create a brighter future. Fresh start aims to be the source of knowledge, information, training, and healing in all matters of emotional and mental health within the Jewish community.Additional initiatives currently underway include training, community lectures, support groups, and the world's first and only Think Tank specifically dedicated to trauma and mental health in the Jewish community. Our approach includes awareness (community lectures, support groups and published materials), advocacy (educational, professional and rabbinical training) and action (retreats, parenting workshops and referral networks).
